71.reason: [Comparing string literals with != is invalid in C; should use strcmp or check length instead. Also, after processing, empty strings may be stored due to consecutive non-alpha characters, leading to output of empty words.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing string literals with != is invalid in C; should use strcmp or check length instead. Also, after processing, empty strings may be stored due to consecutive non-alpha characters, leading to output of empty words.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing a char array with string literals using != is invalid in C; this condition will always evaluate to true because it compares addresses, not contents. Also, empty strings are already handled by logic above, so this check is unnecessary and incorrect.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing string literals with != is invalid in C; should use strcmp or check length instead. Also, words[i].word is an array, not a pointer, so this comparison is always true and meaningless.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing a char array with string literals using != is invalid in C; this condition always evaluates to true and does not check for empty strings correctly. Should use strlen or check first character instead.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Comparing a char array with string literals using != is invalid in C; should use strcmp or check length/first character instead]
